Data source
Alerts

🇺🇸 FDA Enforcement Reports

US Food & Drug Administration mandatory recall reports. Covers food, dietary supplements, cosmetics. Severity: Class I (serious health risk), Class II (temporary adverse health consequences), Class III (unlikely to cause health problems). ~15,900 alerts in database.

🇺🇸 USDA FSIS Recalls

US Department of Agriculture Food Safety and Inspection Service. Covers meat, poultry, and egg products. Same Class I/II/III severity system as FDA. ~900 alerts in database.

🇬🇧 FSA UK Food Alerts

UK Food Standards Agency. Alert types: AA (Allergy Alert), PRIN (Product Recall Information Notice), FAFA (Food Alert for Action — highest urgency). ~1,300 alerts in database.

🇪🇺 RASFF (EU)

EU Rapid Alert System for Food and Feed. Covers all 27 EU member states + EEA. Notification types: serious risk, potentially serious, information. Includes origin country and distribution countries per alert. ~5,500 alerts in database (2025–2026).

⬇️ Export Data
ℹ️ About This Dashboard

What is FoodSafe Intelligence?

An open-source intelligence (OSINT) system that automatically collects, ranks, and presents food safety alerts from official regulatory sources worldwide. Built for the Israeli Ministry of Health's Food Risk Management Unit to enable rapid awareness of emerging food safety threats.

How are alerts ranked?

A Bi-Encoder AI model (DistilRoBERTa, 82M parameters) reads each alert's full text and produces a single risk score. It was trained on ~26,000 pairwise comparisons ("Is alert A more dangerous than B?") labeled by an LLM judge. Each alert is encoded once, making scoring fast and scalable.

Training used a 3-way split: 70% train / 15% validation / 15% test. The model achieved 69.3% pairwise accuracy on the test set, vs. 67.3% for the severity-label baseline. Dropout (0.3) prevents overfitting.

🇮🇱 Israel Watch logic

An alert is flagged as Israel-relevant when "Israel" appears in any of: origin country, distribution countries, title, or description. Limitation: this is text-based matching only — it does not consult actual import registries. Many products distributed in Israel may not be captured if not explicitly named.

Score drivers (counterfactual analysis)

For selected top alerts, the system tests 10 hypothetical changes — replacing the hazard, severity, or distribution — and measures how the score would change. The largest changes reveal which factors drive the risk ranking.

Processing pipeline
① Model training (one-time)
📥 Collect sample
~330 alerts
→
🤖 LLM Labeling
26,000 pairwise comparisons
Claude judges A vs B
→
🧠 Train Bi-Encoder
DistilRoBERTa (BERT)
82M parameters · 3 epochs
→
✅ Trained model
69.3% pairwise accuracy
vs 67.3% baseline

📥 Collect
FDA · FSIS · FSA UK · RASFF
→
🗄️ Store
SQLite DB
→
⚡ Score
Bi-Encoder AI
(trained model)
→
📊 Rank
Percentile tiers
→
📋 Report
This dashboard
What appears in this dashboard?

Alerts are ranked by the Bi-Encoder score within the current time window and assigned to tiers by percentile:

Tier Percentile (within window) Visibility
CRITICAL Top 5% Always visible, expanded by default (first 10)
HIGH Top 5–20% Always visible (first 10 shown)
MEDIUM Top 20–50% Hidden by default — click toggle to show
LOW Bottom 50% Not shown (stored in database only)
🇮🇱 Israel Any Always shown in Israel Watch — score does not matter

⚠️ Tiers are relative to the current window — a "Critical" alert is the most severe among alerts in this period. The X.X/10 score is absolute: normalized from the model's output range (−5.65 to +6.71) and is comparable across time periods. RASFF alerts were not included in model training; their scores are extrapolated.

⚠️ Data coverage note: This system monitors official regulatory sources in the US, UK, and EU. It does not monitor Israeli domestic alerts, Israeli import registries, or Asian/South American regulatory bodies. The Israel Watch section captures only alerts where Israel is explicitly mentioned in a foreign source.
FoodSafe Intelligence · Built for the Israeli Ministry of Health, Food Risk Management Unit · Hebrew University of Jerusalem MBA Capstone 2026 · Generated
